product_overview: OmniBurger POS Suite v2 is a modular single-page application for QSR operations that unifies POS, Kiosk, KDS, TV status board and Admin backoffice with realtime synchronization. It supports role-based access, PIN login and a Supabase-backed realtime backend with a local mock fallback for development.
core_goals: Provide a unified, realtime platform to replace paper/thermal workflows and centralize order lifecycle across POS, Kiosk, KDS and TV.
Reduce customer wait times via self-service kiosk and accurate kitchen production management (KDS).
Enable robust store operations: shift/session financial control, inventory tracking, promotions engine and RBAC administration.
Support multi-terminal concurrency with Supabase Realtime and an offline/mock backend for development resilience.

features.name: PIN Login
features.description: Operator selects a user from the launcher and authenticates with a 4-digit PIN to start an authenticated session and access modules according to role.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Select a user from the user list -> Type four PIN digits using the on-screen keypad -> See successful login and module selection menu
Navigate to / -> Select a user from the user list -> Type incorrect PIN digits using the on-screen keypad -> See invalid PIN feedback -> PIN entry resets for retry

features.name: Module Selection (Launcher)
features.description: After PIN authentication the operator chooses which operational module to open (POS, Kiosk, KDS, TV, Admin). Role-based access prevents unauthorized modules from opening and 'Trocar Módulo' / logout return to the launcher.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See module cards listed (POS, Kiosk, KDS, TV, Admin) -> Tap POS module card -> Open /pos module
Navigate to / -> See module cards listed (POS, Kiosk, KDS, TV, Admin) -> Tap Admin module card as non-ADMIN user -> See access denied message -> Remain on module selection
While in /pos -> Tap 'Trocar Módulo' -> Return to / launcher -> Tap logout button -> See login screen at /

features.name: POS Sales Flow
features.description: Cashier opens/closes shift, builds a cart by adding products and modifiers, processes payment and creates persistent orders, with transaction records stored to Supabase (or mock backend).
user_flows: Navigate to /pos -> Open cashier shift with operator and terminal details -> Filter or search products -> Add product to cart -> Adjust item quantity or modifiers in cart -> Open payment modal -> Select payment method and enter amount -> Confirm payment -> See success modal and order persisted
Navigate to /pos -> Attempt to add product or checkout with no open shift -> See validation error 'Open a shift first' -> Open shift -> Retry checkout

Navigate to /pos -> Add products to cart -> Open payment modal -> Select card payment -> Confirm payment -> Payment declined by gateway/mock -> See payment error message -> Return to payment modal for retry

features.name: Kitchen Display Operations (KDS)
features.description: Kitchen staff views incoming paid orders, advances production status via bump/recall/partial operations, monitors per-order SLA timers and receives realtime updates from the orders stream.
user_flows: Navigate to /kds -> See list of pending/paid orders -> Select a pending order -> Bump order to PREPARING -> Bump order to READY -> See order status update visually and via realtime stream
Navigate to /kds -> See list of PREPARING orders with SLA timers -> Advance order item to PARTIAL for partial delivery -> See PARTIAL reflected in order and on TV/pos via realtime update

Navigate to /kds -> Receive realtime subscription error or network disconnect -> See offline banner or mock-mode indicator -> Attempt bump -> See local validation preventing status change until connection restored

features.name: Kiosk Self-Service Flow
features.description: Customer-facing kiosk provides a splash screen, menu browsing, product customization, cart review and channel-configured payment methods, with privacy timeout and session validation.
user_flows: Navigate to /kiosk -> Touch Splash screen to start -> Browse categories -> Select a product from grid -> Customize modifiers and notes -> Add customized product to cart -> Open cart -> Proceed to payment -> Select payment method -> Confirm payment -> See success/receipt screen
Navigate to /kiosk -> Touch Splash screen to start -> Add items to cart -> Inactivity for 60 seconds on payment screen -> See automatic return to Splash screen
Navigate to /kiosk -> Touch Splash screen to start -> Attempt to checkout while no active StoreSession -> See validation error 'Store session required' -> Cannot complete payment

features.name: Status TV (Public Order Board)
features.description: Read-only display for customers showing orders grouped by status (READY/PRIORITY, PREPARING), with adaptive layout for orientation and visual highlighting for SLA breaches.
user_flows: Navigate to /tv -> See sections 'Prontos' and 'Em Preparo' with orders populated -> Observe overdue orders highlighted and READY orders in green -> See screen adapt to landscape orientation
Navigate to /tv -> No orders available -> See empty-state message prompting 'Nenhum pedido no momento' -> Screen continues polling or listening to realtime updates
Navigate to /tv -> Realtime subscription disconnects -> See connectivity indicator or offline banner -> Continue displaying last known state until reconnect

features.name: Backoffice Administration
features.description: Admin users manage store settings, users, products, promotions, stock and perform operational actions such as opening/closing store sessions and triggering resets; all CRUD and operational actions persist to Supabase (or mock adapter).
user_flows: Navigate to /admin -> Open Users / Scouts / Products section -> Click 'Create' -> Fill required fields and assign role -> Save record -> See success confirmation and record persisted

Navigate to /admin -> Open Store Session controls -> Attempt to close store session while a shift is open -> See validation error 'Cannot close session with open shifts' -> Resolve open shifts -> Close store session successfully
Navigate to /admin -> Open Promotions or Products -> Edit promotion validity or product availability -> Save change -> See immediate effect in POS/Kiosk via realtime sync

routes.description: Login screen (user selection + PIN) and module launcher after authentication.
Cashier terminal for shift handling, cart, checkout, and order creation.
Kitchen Display System for order production status.
Public order status board for customers.
Backoffice administration for store settings, users, and management tools.

features.name: PIN Login
features.description: Operator chooses a user and authenticates by entering a 4-digit PIN.
user_interactions: Select a user from the user list.
Type four PIN digits using the on-screen keypad.
See successful login and module selection menu.
See invalid PIN feedback and PIN reset on incorrect entry.

features.name: Module Selection
features.description: Logged-in user selects which operational module to open, with role-based access.
user_interactions: Tap module cards such as POS, Kiosk, KDS, TV, or Admin.
Use "Trocar Módulo" to return to launcher.
Use logout button to end current session.

features.name: POS Sales Flow
features.description: Cashier opens shift, adds items to cart, confirms payment, and creates order.
user_interactions: Open cashier shift with operator and terminal details.
Add products to cart and adjust item quantities.
Open payment modal and confirm payment method/amount.
View success modal and continue next sale.

api_calls.endpoint: Supabase tables (orders, shifts, store_sessions) via backend adapter

features.name: Kitchen Display Operations
features.description: Kitchen staff monitors incoming orders and updates production status.
user_interactions: Advance order status based on preparation step.
Monitor real-time updates from order stream.

features.name: Backoffice Administration
features.description: Admin manages store controls, order operations, and scout/user management.
user_interactions: Navigate admin sections and manage business data.
Create/update records such as users/scouts/catalog items.
Trigger operational actions like session/shift controls.

api_calls.endpoint: Supabase tables (products, users, scouts, promotions, settings)

known_limitations.issue: Route navigation is implemented as in-memory component state (no URL-based router), so module deep-linking and browser history are limited.
known_limitations.impact: Automated navigation must interact through the launcher UI instead of direct URL routes.

known_limitations.issue: If Supabase environment variables are missing, app falls back to local mock backend mode.
known_limitations.impact: End-to-end persistence/realtime behavior differs from production backend.
